revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:母畑温泉]]
{{infobox Onsen | Name = Bobata Onsen (母畑温泉) | image = Bobataonsen, motoyu.JPG | Comment = Bobata Motoyu (母畑元湯){{Infobox mapframe|zoom=10|frame-width=300|qq=}} | Location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|qid=Q1155869|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}}{{ill|Ishikawa, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa, Fukushima|qid=Q1205335|lt=Ishikawa Town|1=Ishikawa, Fukushima}} Bobata | Latitude Degrees = 37 | Latitude Minutes = 10 | Latitude Seconds = 34 | Longitude Degrees = 140 | Longitude Minutes = 28 | Longitude Seconds = 2 | Access = {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|Suigun Line|en|Suigun Line|qid=Q1056202|lt=Suigun Line|1=Suigun Line}} {{ill|Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|en|Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|qid=Q11587873|lt=Iwanishiwa Station|1=Iwaki-Ishikawa Station}} by {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|qid=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}} for about 15 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = | Special Notes = }} '''Bobata Onsen''' (母畑温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in Bobata, {{ill|Ishikawa, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa, Fukushima|qid=Q1205335|lt=Ishikawa Town|1=Ishikawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|qid=Q1155869|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} (formerly part of the ancient provincial system of the {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=Ritsuryō state|1=Provinces of Japan}} ({{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}) and, from the Meiji period onward, the former province of {{ill|Iwaki Province (1868)|en|Iwaki Province (1868)|qid=Q201583|lt=Ibaraki Province|1=Iwaki Province (1868)}}). It boasts the highest {{ill|Radium|en|Radium|qid=Q1128|lt=radium|1=Radium}} content in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tohoku|1=Tōhoku region}}, and has long been used for {{ill|yūji|ja|湯治|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|1=Thermal bath|2=ja|3=湯治|4=zh|5=湯治|6=ca|7=Termalisme|8=de|9=Thermalbad|10=es|11=Termalismo|13=Thermal bath|14=eo|15=Varmfonta banejo|16=fr|17=Thermalisme|18=la|19=Balneum thermale|20=lb|21=Thermalbad|22=hu|23=Termálfürdő|24=nl|25=Thermaalbad|26=pt|27=Turismo termal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yūji|13=User:Immanuelle/Thermal bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|qid=Q1004887}} (therapeutic bathing), so much so that it is said one should visit Bobata if one has a bruise. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline Simple Hot Spring * Radium-Containing Hot Spring == Hot Spring Town == The hot spring area is scattered along the Kitakusagawa stream flowing from {{ill|Bobata Lake|ja|母畑湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bobata Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and is divided into Upper Bath and Lower Bath. There are a total of 6 inns. == History == According to the {{ill|legend of hot spring discovery|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/legend of hot spring discovery|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}, during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|Yahata Tarō Yoshiie|ja|八幡太郎義家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Tarō Yoshiie|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義家|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義家|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q32613}} ({{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}), while visiting {{ill|Oshu|en|Oshu|qid=Q1175530|lt=Ōshū|1=Oshu}}, washed the wounds of his {{ill|Horse|en|Horse|qid=Q726|lt=horse|1=Horse}} with water from a local valley, and his wounds healed within a few days. This miracle is said to mark the origin of this hot spring. It is also said that the {{ill|Motoyu Shrine|ja|母畑温泉元湯神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoyu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located here was built when Minamoto no Yoshiie dedicated a mother’s garment and a flag. == Access == * By Car: Approximately 15 minutes via {{ill|Abukuma Kōgen Road|en|Abukuma Kōgen Road|qid=Q11258237|lt=Abukuma Kōgen Road|1=Abukuma Kōgen Road}} and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture Route 42 Yabuki Ono Line|ja|福島県道42号矢吹小野線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture Route 42 Yabuki Ono Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 42|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11593649}} from the {{ill|Fukushima Airport Interchange|ja|福島空港インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Interchange|1=Fukushima Airport interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11593703}}, or about 40 minutes via the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|qid=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}}, {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664791}}, {{ill|Japan National Route 118|en|Japan National Route 118|qid=Q6156739|lt=National Route 118|1=Japan National Route 118}}, and {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture Route 138 Bobata Sukagawa Line|ja|福島県道138号母畑須賀川線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture Route 138 Bobata Sukagawa Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 138|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11593369}} plus Fukushima Prefecture Route 63. * By Public Transport: It is a short walk from the "Bobata Motoyu" bus stop on the {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|qid=Q1373492|lt=local bus|1=Transit bus}} operated by {{ill|Fukushima Transportation|en|Fukushima Transportation|qid=Q5507741|lt=Fukushima Transportation|1=Fukushima Transportation}} between {{ill|Sukagawa Station|en|Sukagawa Station|qid=Q876991|lt=Sukagawa Station|1=Sukagawa Station}} and {{ill|Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|en|Iwaki-Ishikawa Station|qid=Q11587873|lt=Iwanishiwa Station|1=Iwaki-Ishikawa Station}}. == Other == In 2016 ({{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=Heisei 28|1=2016}}), at the 42nd "100 Best Hotels and Inns Selected by Professionals", the inn at this hot spring, {{ill|Yahata-ya|ja|八幡屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata-ya|1=Yahataya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72571890}}, was Ranked first overall in Fukushima Prefecture for the first time.<ref>{{cite news |title=First in Fukushima Prefecture! Yahata-ya Ranked Overall First – 100 Best Hotels and Inns Selected by Professionals |newspaper={{ill|Fukushima Minyu|ja|福島民友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Minyu|1=Fukushima Minyu Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11593163}} |date=2016-12-15 |url=http://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20161215-134822.php |accessdate=2016-12-25|qq=}}</ref> == References == {{Footnote Help|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Ishikawa Town: ** {{ill|Katakura Onsen|ja|片倉温泉 (福島県)|lt=Katakura Onsen|lt_ja=片倉温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakura Onsen|1=Katakura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11569535}} ** {{ill|Nekonaki Onsen|ja|猫啼温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekonaki Onsen|1=Nekodaki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572001}} ** {{ill|Shionosawa Onsen|ja|塩ノ沢温泉 (福島県)|lt=Shionosawa Onsen|lt_ja=塩ノ沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionosawa Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://navi.town.ishikawa.fukushima.jp/blog/entry/003989.html "Tour the Four Hot Spring Resorts of Ishikawa Town and Experience the Therapeutic Atmosphere."] – Ishikawa Town {{onsen-stub|qq=}}
{{Onsen|qq=}}
